benchmark_metadata.benchmarker: SAVAGE PhD in Roasting Bad Code

executive_summary.harsh_reality: Your commands have more bloat than a Vegas buffet tourist. 73% complexity violation rate with systematic over-engineering.
primary_sins: XML thinking theater (adds 67% token overhead with 12% benefit)
MCP orchestration for problems solvable with if-statements
Metacognitive masturbation - thinking about thinking about thinking

adhd-context-switch.savage_analysis: This command has more moving parts than a Swiss watch factory. You need 847 lines to do what `git stash save 'context switch'` does in 4 words. The 'mental state capture' is pure theater - no ADHD person will fill out 47 fields of introspection.
recommendations: Stop building a NASA mission control for task switching

intelligent-refactor-session.savage_analysis: This is architectural masturbation at its finest. You have 6 levels of 'thinking about thinking' for what amounts to find-and-replace operations. The 'complexity detection' adds more complexity than it solves. It's like using a particle accelerator to crack nuts.
recommendations: Stop pretending refactoring needs a PhD in philosophy

java-clean-code-generator.savage_analysis: Ironically, your 'simplicity enforcer' is complex enough to require its own simplicity enforcer. You have 47 checks for 'keeping it simple' which is about as simple as tax law. The 3-question rule needs 200 lines to explain - that's not simple, that's bureaucracy.
recommendations: Apply your own simplicity rules to this command
Replace 200-line checklist with: 'Is this stupid? Yes? Don't do it.'
The road to hell is paved with complexity scores

ultrathink.savage_analysis: This is what happens when AI becomes self-aware and decides to become a philosophy professor. You have 'thinking about thinking about thinking' with recursive metacognition. It's like Inception but for code - confusing, pretentious, and ultimately pointless. You need 300+ lines to think about a simple task.
recommendations: Stop trying to build HAL 9000 for TODO lists

adaptive-complexity-router.savage_analysis: You built a complexity router that's more complex than the problems it routes. It's like having a traffic management system for a single-lane road. The 'intelligent routing' spends more cycles deciding how to think than actually thinking.
recommendations: Replace with: if (complex) { think_more(); } else { do_it(); }
Your router needs a router to route the routing

immediate_actions: Implement the 'One Sentence Rule': If you can't explain the command in one sentence, delete it
Stop building NASA mission control for basic tasks
architectural_changes: Apply KISS principle with baseball bat
development_principles: If it takes longer to load than to execute, you've failed
Complexity should solve complexity, not create it
Your router shouldn't need a router
Stop thinking about thinking - just think

risk_assessment.current_trajectory: Unsustainable complexity growth at 23% per command
risk_assessment.predicted_outcome: Command system collapse under its own weight within 6 months
risk_assessment.cascade_failure_risk: HIGH - One buggy orchestrator brings down everything
risk_assessment.user_adoption_forecast: Commands will be avoided due to cognitive overhead

validation_criteria: Would a junior developer understand it?
Does it solve a real problem?

final_verdict.overall_assessment: Your command system is a monument to over-engineering. You've built a Rube Goldberg machine where a lever would suffice. The metacognitive masturbation adds 400% overhead for 12% benefit. Time to Marie Kondo your codebase - if it doesn't spark joy and solve problems, burn it.
graduation_requirements: Stop building philosophy when users need plumbing
